summary: Completed frontend visual regression testing after CSS variables implementation for dark/light theme toggle. All main pages render correctly with theme-adaptive styling.

issues: Line 79: Hardcoded color #3A4A5C still used for star rating empty state - should use var(--theme-border)
Line 156: Hardcoded gradient colors #1E2A38 and #253545 - should use CSS variables
Line 555: Hardcoded gradient colors #0F1923 and #1E2A38 - should use CSS variables

passed_tests: Landing page renders correctly with dark theme (background: rgb(15, 17, 21))
Logo has golden glow effect via drop-shadow filter: rgba(246, 195, 106, 0.6)
Theme toggle button (sun/moon icon) present with data-testid='theme-toggle'
Dark mode: No 'light-mode' class on html element
Light mode: 'light-mode' class added correctly on toggle
CSS variables change correctly on theme toggle
Login page renders with themed card and logo glow (background: rgb(15, 17, 21))
Registration page renders with themed tabs and form
Subscriptions page (/subscriptions) renders 8 plan cards with pricing in EUR
Blog page (/blog) renders with 49 themed article cards
About page (/about) renders with 7 sections and themed CTAs
Services page (/services) shows auth gate for non-authenticated users (expected behavior)
Navigation links (nav-home, nav-calculator, nav-companies, nav-more-btn) all work
More dropdown opens correctly with subscriptions, blog, about, services links
Footer renders correctly with themed background
Live counter component present with themed styling
action_items: MINOR: Replace hardcoded colors in FeedbackButton.jsx, PublishedGalleryPage.jsx, Scanner3DPage.jsx with CSS variables for full theme consistency

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All main pages render correctly with CSS variables

context_for_next_testing_agent: CSS variables implementation verified. Dark/light theme toggle works. Services page requires authentication (AuthGate wrapper). Minor hardcoded colors found in 3 components (FeedbackButton, PublishedGalleryPage, Scanner3DPage) but all main pages use CSS variables correctly.
